title: Caught this move today   still trying to figure out how much was strategy vs timing
author: Rude-Anywhere2712
selftext:
Today turned out better than expected.

The move came pretty quickly, and honestly it’s one of those situations where I’m still figuring out how much was actual strategy vs just being in the right place at the right time.

I’ve been focusing more on timing entries around momentum shifts rather than holding blindly, and it seems to be helping   but days like this always make me question what’s repeatable and what isn’t.

comments.author: PostAntique8408
comments.body: I'm very interested in your strategy

replies.author: toanthrax
replies.body: Exactly. Fucking scammers. Also they guys who comment that they are interested adding to the bait.

replies.author: Six2guy
replies.body: They make it so easy to spot.

comments.author: Fire-Engineering
comments.body:
This is a classic pump-and-dump in disguise.
The scammer builds trust by posting calm, “disciplined” content about small-cap stocks, moving averages, and free watchlists. Once they gain a following, they quietly push specific tickers they already own (or get paid to promote). Retail buyers pile in, pumping the price. The scammer then dumps their shares at the top, crashes the stock, and repeats the cycle with the next one.
All wrapped in fake humility to look like a helpful trader.
